hypotheses: The DUT outputs for the don't-care input combinations (3, 8, 11, 12) are not matching the reference model, which expects logic-0 for these cases, leading to persistent mismatches [evidence: log_excerpt L8 'passes on the 12 required input combinations, but doesn't match the don't-care cases'], [evidence: attempt_history patch_summaries].

Despite explicit enumeration in the RTL, there may be a mismatch in the mapping of input vectors to output values for the don't-care cases, possibly due to an off-by-one or encoding error in the case statement [evidence: RTL L17-22, patch_summaries].

The testbench expects deterministic (logic-0) outputs for all 16 input combinations, but the DUT may still be producing X or undefined values for some don't-care cases, which are counted as mismatches [evidence: log_excerpt L10-L12, testbench L154-155].

The repeated failure signature and mismatch count (10 mismatches) suggest that the same set of input combinations are failing in each attempt, indicating that the patch strategy has not addressed the root cause [evidence: attempt_history, log_excerpt L15, stagnation flags].
likely_failure_points: The case statement in the RTL (L17-22) may not be correctly covering all 16 input combinations, or the default branch may not be triggered as expected for the don't-care cases [evidence: RTL L17-22, patch_summaries].

Assignment to sop_reg and pos_reg in the always block may not be initializing outputs to logic-0 for all unspecified cases, possibly due to synthesis/simulation differences or missed input patterns [evidence: RTL L21, log_excerpt L8, L10].

The testbench's comparison logic (tb_match) is strict and will count any X or undefined output from the DUT as a mismatch, so any uninitialized or floating output will cause persistent failures [evidence: testbench L154-155, log_excerpt L10-L12].
recommended_probes: Add a $display statement inside the RTL always @(*) block to print the value of 'in', 'sop_reg', and 'pos_reg' for every input combination, especially for in=3,8,11,12, to confirm output assignment [evidence: RTL L17-22].
In the testbench, log the values of a,b,c,d, out_sop_dut, out_pos_dut, out_sop_ref, out_pos_ref at every mismatch to identify which input combinations are failing [evidence: testbench L154-155].
Check the waveform for out_sop and out_pos at times corresponding to the first mismatch (time=170) to see the actual DUT output versus expected [evidence: log_excerpt L10, waveform_excerpt].
Explicitly probe for X or Z values on out_sop and out_pos in the waveform to rule out uninitialized outputs [evidence: testbench L154-155, log_excerpt L10-L12].
analysis_notes: The evidence is strong that the repeated failures are due to the DUT not matching the reference for the don't-care input combinations, which the testbench expects to be logic-0. The RTL attempts to enumerate all 16 input combinations, but the persistent mismatch count and failure signature indicate that either the enumeration is incomplete, or the outputs are not being set deterministically for all cases (possibly due to simulation initialization or a subtle bug in the case statement). The testbench's strict comparison logic will flag any X or undefined output as a mismatch, so it is critical to ensure that all outputs are explicitly assigned for every input. The recommended probes will quickly falsify or confirm whether the outputs are ever X or incorrectly assigned for the don't-care cases. This is a repeated failure signature with stale patch strategies; to break stagnation, direct observation of the DUT outputs for the problematic input combinations is required.
